content.cn_text: 第三部分。你将听到两名叫 Sally
content.en_text: Section 3 You will hear two students called Sally

content.cn_text: 和 Steve 的学生讨论 Sally 关于同伴评估的项目，这是一种学生互相批改
content.en_text: and Steve discussing Sally's project on peer assessment, a system where students mark

content.cn_text: 彼此作业的制度。
content.en_text: each other's work.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第5页的第21至第24题。
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 21 to 24 on page 5.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第24题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 24.

content.cn_text: Steve：真不敢相信你还在图书馆，Sally，你已经在这儿待了一整天了。
content.en_text: Steve: I can't believe you're still in the library Sally, you've been here all day.

content.cn_text: 你还在做你的同伴评估项目吗？
content.en_text: Are you still working on your peer assessment project?

content.cn_text: Sally：嗨，Steve，是的，我还在这儿，而且离完成还差得远。
content.en_text: Sally: Hi Steve, yes I'm still here and I'm nowhere near finished.

content.cn_text: 我还在消化我读过的关于同伴评估的资料。
content.en_text: I'm still processing the reading I've done on peer assessment.

content.cn_text: 我想看看学生互相批改作业是否有益。
content.en_text: I'm interested to see if students marking each other's work is beneficial.

content.cn_text: 我知道很多其他学生非常怀疑它的价值，
content.en_text: I know a lot of the other students are pretty skeptical about its value

content.cn_text: 不认为它是一种评估他们表现的有效方式。
content.en_text: as a way of assessing their performance.

content.cn_text: 他们认为导师支持这种方式，是因为导师不用花那么长时间批改。
content.en_text: They think the tutors are in favour of it because they don't have to spend so long marking.

content.cn_text: 但我不同意，我认为我们能从中学到很多。
content.en_text: But I disagree, I think we can learn a great deal from it.

content.cn_text: Steve：是的，我觉得一个优点是它能节省课堂上讲解内容的时间。
content.en_text: Steve: Yes, I can see one advantage being that it saves time spent going over things in class.

content.cn_text: Sally：嗯，起初看起来并不是这样，而且这可能非常耗时，
content.en_text: Sally: Well initially it doesn't seem to work that way and it can be quite a time consuming business

content.cn_text: 因为如果导师认为学生的评估不可靠，有时作业还得重新批改。
content.en_text: because sometimes things have to be marked again if the tutor thinks the student's assessments aren't reliable.

content.cn_text: 真正的收获是，学生学会退一步客观地评估自己的作业，
content.en_text: The real gain is the fact that the students learn to stand back and assess their own assignments objectively

content.cn_text: 因为他们对评分体系更加熟悉。
content.en_text: because they're much more familiar with the marking system.

content.cn_text: Steve：是的，我明白了，这给了他们一个反思自己表现的机会。
content.en_text: Steve: Yes I can see that, it gives them an opportunity to reflect on their own performance

content.cn_text: 那他们对让另一个学生批改自己的作业感觉如何？
content.en_text: and how do they feel about having another student mark their work?

content.cn_text: 这对建立良好关系有帮助吗？
content.en_text: Is that good for rapport?

content.cn_text: Sally：有些情况下有帮助，有些情况下没有。
content.en_text: Sally: In some cases yes, in some cases no.

content.cn_text: 我认为还需要更多研究来回答这个问题。
content.en_text: I think more research is needed to answer that question.

content.cn_text: Steve：如果导师发现某份作业的分数似乎不对，会怎么样？
content.en_text: Steve: What happens if a tutor notices that the marks for a particular assignment seem wrong?

content.cn_text: 学生应该重新批改吗？
content.en_text: Should the students mark them again?

content.cn_text: Sally：那可能不会有什么不同。
content.en_text: Sally: That might not make any difference.

content.cn_text: 所以在这种情况下，导师必须重新批改所有那些作业，
content.en_text: So in that case the tutor would have to remark all those assignments

content.cn_text: 然后在下一次作业时，结合一些样本答案，和学生仔细讲解评分标准。
content.en_text: and then go through the marking criteria really carefully with the students on the next assignment with some sample answers.

content.cn_text: Steve：明白了。
content.en_text: Steve: Right.

content.cn_text: Sally：我一直在读一个关于同伴评估的研究项目。
content.en_text: Sally: I've been reading about a research project on peer assessment.

content.cn_text: 研究人员邀请了来自两所不同大学的一组学生参加一天的会议。
content.en_text: The researchers invited a group of students from two different universities to attend a one day conference.

content.cn_text: 他们还邀请了一些大学讲师参加。
content.en_text: They also invited some university lecturers to attend.

content.cn_text: 每个学生就自己选择的项目做了一个简短展示。
content.en_text: Each student gave a short presentation on a project of their choice.

content.cn_text: 这些展示中的每一个都由所有参与者单独评估，包括学生和讲师。
content.en_text: Each of these presentations was assessed individually by all the participants, both students and lecturers.

content.cn_text: 为了辅助评分，他们使用了一套评估指南，这套指南由随机选出的一些学生在前一天准备。
content.en_text: To support their marking, they used a set of assessment guidelines that had been prepared the day before by a random selection of students.

content.cn_text: 在听讨论的剩余部分之前，你有一些时间查看第6页的第25至第30题。
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 25 to 30 on page 6.

content.cn_text: 现在请听，并回答第25至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 25 to 30.

content.cn_text: Steve：那么学生评分者可靠吗？
content.en_text: Steve: So were the student markers reliable?

content.cn_text: Sally：嗯，研究比较了某些类别的学生评分者和包括讲师在内的整体小组。
content.en_text: Sally: Well, comparisons were made between certain categories of student markers and the group as a whole, including the lecturers.

content.cn_text: 结果显示，不同群体的评分方式存在一些差异。
content.en_text: The findings showed that there were some variations in the way different groups marked.

content.cn_text: 例如，性别确实起了作用。
content.en_text: Gender did come into play for example.

content.cn_text: Steve：我猜男学生给女展示者评分时会更宽松，是吗？
content.en_text: Steve: I expect the male students were more generous when marking female presenters were they?

content.cn_text: Sally：其实正好相反。他们对女展示者比对男展示者更严格，这也不是我预料到的。
content.en_text: Sally: Quite the reverse actually. They were harder on the female presenters than on the males, which wasn't what I'd expected either.

content.cn_text: Steve：女学生对男展示者有偏向吗？
content.en_text: Steve: Did female students show any bias towards male presenters?

content.cn_text: Sally：有意思的是，没有。
content.en_text: Sally: Interestingly not.

content.cn_text: Steve：那这是否说明女性评分者比男性更公平、更可靠？
content.en_text: Steve: So does that suggest women are fairer and more reliable markers than men?

content.cn_text: Sally：嗯，我不会说到那个程度。这只是一个小样本。
content.en_text: Sally: Well, I wouldn't go that far. It's only a small sample.

content.cn_text: 但重要的是，结果显示，当女性给其他女性评分时，这并没有影响她们给出的分数。
content.en_text: But significantly, the results showed that when women marked other women, this didn't affect the grades they gave.

content.cn_text: Steve：年龄方面呢？你能得出什么结论吗？
content.en_text: Steve: What about age? Were you able to come to any conclusions about that?

content.cn_text: Sally：可以，因为有一些成熟学生做展示，我们发现年轻同伴给他们的分数相当宽松。
content.en_text: Sally: Yes, because we had some mature students presenting and we found the marks awarded to them by their younger peers were rather more generous.

content.cn_text: Steve：哦，我想知道为什么会这样。
content.en_text: Steve: Oh, I wonder why that is.

content.cn_text: 你刚才说有来自两所大学的学生。他们会倾向于给自己大学的展示者更高分吗？
content.en_text: Now, you said there were groups of students from two universities. Did they tend to favour students making presentations from their own universities?

content.cn_text: Sally：这似乎并没有以任何方式影响评分，这相当令人鼓舞。
content.en_text: Sally: They didn't seem to influence marking one way or the other, which is quite encouraging.

content.cn_text: 我们原本想看看学生之间的个人关系是否会影响他们的客观性。
content.en_text: We'd been interested to see if students' personal relationships affected their objectivity.

content.cn_text: 不过，一个显著差异出现在那些被要求帮助制定评分标准的学生身上。
content.en_text: One striking variation though was in the students who'd been asked to help devise the criteria for the marking scheme.

content.cn_text: 他们实际上是所有人中最严格的评分者，给分一直低于导师。
content.en_text: They were actually the toughest markers of all, marking consistently below the tutors.

content.cn_text: Steve：真的吗？嗯，听起来它产生了很多有用的数据。
content.en_text: Steve: Really? Well, it sounds as if it's generated lots of useful data.

content.cn_text: 第三部分到此结束。现在你有半分钟检查答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 3. You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 现在请翻到第7页第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section 4 on page 7.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
groups.title: Questions 21–24

items.title: Sally says many students see peer assessment as

options.title: A
options.content: a way for tutors to save time.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题问 Sally 说很多学生如何看待同伴评估。原文说他们认为导师喜欢这种方式是因为 don't have to spend so long marking，也就是导师省时间，所以答案是 A. a way for tutors to save time。

items.title: What do Steve and Sally agree is an advantage of peer assessment?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第22题问 Steve 和 Sally 都同意同伴评估有什么优点。Sally 说学生能 objectively assess their own assignments，Steve 也说 gives them an opportunity to reflect on their own performance，所以答案是 B. It increases students’ self-awareness。

items.title: If the peer assessment marks seem incorrect,

options.title: A
options.content: students should mark the assignments again.

options.title: B
options.content: the tutor should check those assignments.

options.title: C
options.content: the marking criteria should be rewritten.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题问如果同伴评估分数看起来不正确，该怎么办。原文说 the tutor would have to remark all those assignments，所以答案是 B. the tutor should check those assignments。

items.title: How were the presentations in the research project marked?

options.title: A
options.content: Students and lecturers marked all the presentations.

options.title: B
options.content: Students chose which presentations they wanted to mark.

options.title: C
options.content: Lecturers marked a selection of presentations.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第24题问研究项目中的展示是如何评分的。原文说 assessed by all the participants, both students and lecturers，所以答案是 A. Students and lecturers marked all the presentations。

groups.desc: Write the correct letter, A, B or C, next to questions 25–30.
groups.title: Questions 25–30

list.title: C
list.content: Their marks were not significantly different

items.title: male students marking female presenters ………………
items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问男学生给女展示者评分时与整体相比如何。原文说 they were harder on the female presenters，说明给分更低，所以答案是 B. They gave lower marks。

items.title: female students marking male presenters ………………
items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问女学生给男展示者评分时与整体相比如何。原文问是否有 bias，回答 interestingly not，说明没有显著差异，所以答案是 C. Their marks were not significantly different。

items.title: female students marking female presenters ………………
items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题问女学生给女展示者评分时与整体相比如何。原文说 this didn't affect the grades they gave，说明没有显著差异，所以答案是 C. Their marks were not significantly different。

items.title: younger students marking older presenters ………………
items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题问年轻学生给年长展示者评分时与整体相比如何。原文说 marks awarded to them by younger peers were rather more generous，说明给分更高，所以答案是 A. They gave higher marks。

items.title: students marking presenters from a different university ………………
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题问学生给来自不同大学的展示者评分时与整体相比如何。原文说 didn't seem to influence marking one way or the other，说明没有显著差异，所以答案是 C. Their marks were not significantly different。

items.title: students who had helped set criteria ………………
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题问帮助制定评分标准的学生与整体相比如何。原文说 they were the toughest markers，并且 marking below the tutors，说明给分更低，所以答案是 B. They gave lower marks。
